title: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 2
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14-26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

Delayed Adulthood

A

In many wealthy countries, the familiar markers of adulthood are arriving later than they did for previous generations. Marriage, home ownership, parenthood and secure career status are still important to many young people, but they often happen after a longer period of study, renting, changing jobs or living with family. The phrase delayed adulthood can sound like criticism, as if young adults are simply refusing to grow up. Yet researchers argue that the delay is better understood as a response to changing economic and social conditions. The question is not only what young people want, but also what has become harder to obtain.

B

One reason is the expansion of education. More young adults now spend their early twenties completing degrees, professional training or unpaid internships. Dr Lena Ortiz, a developmental psychologist, says this does not mean that they lack seriousness. According to Ortiz, delayed adulthood is often a planning stage in which young people collect qualifications before taking on long-term commitments. She argues that the old sequence of school, job, marriage and home no longer fits a labour market that rewards extended preparation. In her view, the delay is not a rejection of responsibility, but a different route towards it.

C

Housing has become a major barrier. Professor Mark Feld, a housing economist, points out that in many cities rents have risen faster than wages, making it harder to save for a deposit. He says young adults may want independence, but independence is expensive when rent consumes much of a monthly income. Feld also notes that home ownership once gave people a sense of progress, whereas today it may be postponed for years even by graduates with full-time jobs. For him, delayed home ownership is less about lifestyle preference than about the arithmetic of prices, debt and insecure earnings.

D

Relationships and family formation have changed as well. Sociologist Maya Singh argues that later marriage should not automatically be interpreted as fear of commitment. Many young adults, she says, want partnerships based on emotional maturity, equality and shared domestic work. Singh believes that women in particular may delay marriage or children until they feel that a relationship will not require them to sacrifice education or employment. She also observes that reliable childcare and parental leave policies affect decisions about when to have children. In societies where care remains costly, parenthood may be delayed even when people want families.

E

Career stability is another uncertain milestone. Dr Jonah Reed, a labour-market researcher, says that early employment has become more fragmented. Short contracts, platform work and repeated job changes can leave young people employed but not settled. Reed argues that older measures of adulthood assumed a stable job would arrive soon after education, but many young workers now spend years building a career from temporary positions. He warns against calling this laziness, because the structure of entry-level work has changed. A person may be working hard while still lacking the security needed for marriage, a mortgage or children.

F

Family support can both reduce and hide the problem. Some young adults live with parents in order to save money or avoid debt. This arrangement may be practical, but it can also make inequality less visible. Young people whose families can provide housing, tuition or emergency money often have more room to delay risky decisions. Those without support may enter marriage, work or parenthood earlier, not because they feel more prepared, but because they have fewer options. Delayed adulthood therefore varies sharply by class, region and family wealth.

G

The trend has consequences for society. Later household formation can reduce birth rates, change demand for schools and housing, and delay the transfer of wealth between generations. At the same time, a slower transition can allow some people to choose partners more carefully, gain skills and avoid commitments that would be difficult to reverse. Researchers therefore caution against treating delayed adulthood as either a moral failure or a simple sign of freedom. It is a pattern created by personal choice, social expectation and material constraint, all operating together.

Questions 14-20

Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A-G.

Which paragraph contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A-G, in boxes 14-20 on your answer sheet.

14 an explanation of purchasing property offered
15 reference to education extending the period before long-term commitments

16 reference to postponed adult milestones

17 mention of how access to affordable nursery care and statutory leave shapes timing of parenthood

18 a reference to piecemeal time off secure

19 an explanation of family support adult milestones less difficult

20 examples of family home setup

Questions 21-26

Look at the following statements (Questions 21-26) and the list of people below.

Match each statement with the correct person, A-D.

Write the correct letter, A-D, in boxes 21-26 on your answer sheet.

NB   You may use any letter more than once.

21 a reference to postponed adult milestones

22 a reference to wedding property workforce economy favours training

23 a reference to costs down payment

24 a reference to wedding dedication

25 a reference to how access to affordable nursery care and statutory leave shapes timing of parenthood

26 a reference to workforce economy piecemeal time off secure

List of People

A Dr Lena Ortiz

B Professor Mark Feld

C Maya Singh

D Dr Jonah Reed
rawAnswerKey:
Question 14: an explanation of purchasing property offered

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: home ownership once gave people a sense of progress, whereas today it may be postponed for years even by graduates with full-time jobs.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะย่อหน้า C อธิบาย housing barrier โดยบอกว่า rents rise faster than wages ทำให้ saving for a deposit ยากขึ้น และ home ownership อาจถูก postponed for years even by graduates with full-time jobs โจทย์ถามว่าทำไมการซื้อบ้านยากขึ้นแม้แต่กับ employed graduates ซึ่งย่อหน้า C ตอบเรื่อง prices, rent, deposit, debt และ insecure earnings ชัดเจน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: purchasing property offered = home ownership once gave people sense progress whereas; answer signal = C



Question 15: reference to education extending the period before long-term commitments

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: More young adults now spend their early twenties completing degrees, professional training or unpaid internships.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะย่อหน้า B กล่าวถึง expansion of education และบอกว่าคนหนุ่มสาวใช้ช่วง early twenties ไปกับ degrees, professional training หรือ unpaid internships ก่อนจะรับ long-term commitments โจทย์ถาม education ที่ขยายช่วงเวลาก่อน commitment ระยะยาว ซึ่งสอดคล้องกับ planning stage และ collecting qualifications ในย่อหน้า B

Paraphrased Vocabulary: education = degrees / professional training / internships; extending the period = spend their early twenties; before commitments = before taking on long-term commitments; answer signal = B

Question 16: reference to postponed adult milestones

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: The phrase delayed adulthood can sound like criticism, as if young adults are simply refusing to grow up. Yet researchers argue that the delay is better understood as a response to changing economic and social conditions.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะย่อหน้า A อธิบายว่า delayed adulthood มักฟังดูเหมือนคำวิจารณ์ว่าคนหนุ่มสาวไม่ยอมโต แต่ผู้วิจัยบอกว่าควรเข้าใจว่าเป็นผลจาก economic and social conditions มากกว่า โจทย์ถามการมอง delayed adulthood แบบผิด ๆ ว่าเป็น personal failure ซึ่งตรงกับ as if young adults are simply refusing to grow up และการแก้ความเข้าใจผิดในย่อหน้า A

Paraphrased Vocabulary: postponed adult milestones = phrase delayed adulthood sound like criticism young adults; answer signal = A



Question 17: mention of how access to affordable nursery care and statutory leave shapes timing of parenthood

Correct Answer: D

Accepted Answers: D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: reliable childcare and parental leave policies affect decisions about when to have children.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ D เพราะย่อหน้า D ระบุชัดว่า reliable childcare and parental leave policies affect decisions about when to have children โจทย์ถาม childcare และ leave policies ที่ส่งผลต่อการตัดสินใจเรื่อง parenthood ซึ่งเป็นข้อความตรงจากย่อหน้า D จึงตอบ D ไม่ใช่ย่อหน้าอื่นที่พูดเรื่อง housing หรือ career

Paraphrased Vocabulary: care support parental leave = reliable childcare parental leave policies affect decisions children; answer signal = D



Question 18: a reference to piecemeal time off secure

Correct Answer: E

Accepted Answers: E

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: early employment has become more fragmented. Short contracts, platform work and repeated job changes can leave young people employed but not settled.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ E เพราะย่อหน้า E กล่าวถึง career stability โดยบอกว่า early employment has become more fragmented และยกตัวอย่าง short contracts, platform work และ repeated job changes โจทย์ถาม early work ที่ less stable than in the past ซึ่งตรงกับ employed but not settled และการเปรียบเทียบกับ older measures of adulthood ในย่อหน้า E

Paraphrased Vocabulary: piecemeal time off secure = early employment become fragmented short contracts platform work; answer signal = E



Question 19: an explanation of family support adult milestones less difficult

Correct Answer: F

Accepted Answers: F

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: Young people whose families can provide housing, tuition or emergency money often have more room to delay risky decisions.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ F เพราะย่อหน้า F อธิบายว่าครอบครัวที่ให้ housing, tuition หรือ emergency money ได้ ทำให้คนหนุ่มสาวมี more room to delay risky decisions นี่ตรงกับโจทย์ที่ถามว่า family resources ทำให้ delayed adulthood ง่ายขึ้นสำหรับบางคนอย่างไร ย่อหน้านี้ยังเชื่อมกับ inequality และ class จึงเป็นคำตอบที่ชัดเจนที่สุด

Paraphrased Vocabulary: adult milestones less difficult = young people whose families provide housing tuition emergency; answer signal = F



Question 20: examples of family home setup

Correct Answer: G

Accepted Answers: G

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: Later household formation can reduce birth rates, change demand for schools and housing, and delay the transfer of wealth between generations.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ G เพราะย่อหน้า G ยกตัวอย่าง consequences ของ later household formation ได้แก่ reduce birth rates, change demand for schools and housing และ delay the transfer of wealth between generations โจทย์ถาม social effects ที่เกิดจากการสร้าง household ช้าลง ซึ่งตรงกับรายการผลกระทบในย่อหน้า G โดยตรง

Paraphrased Vocabulary: family home setup = later household formation reduce birth rates change demand; answer signal = G



Question 21: a reference to postponed adult milestones

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: According to Ortiz, delayed adulthood is often a planning stage in which young people collect qualifications before taking on long-term commitments. She argues that the old sequence of school, job, marriage and home no longer fits a labour market that rewards extended preparation. In her view, the delay is not a rejection of responsibility, but a different route towards it.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะ statement นี้ตรงกับความเห็นของ Dr Lena Ortiz ที่บอกว่า delayed adulthood เป็น planning stage และ young people collect qualifications ก่อน taking on long-term commitments เธอยังพูดชัดว่า delay is not a rejection of responsibility แต่เป็น different route towards it ดังนั้นผู้พูดคือ Ortiz ซึ่งอยู่ในตัวเลือก A

Paraphrased Vocabulary: postponed adult milestones = according ortiz delayed adulthood often planning stage young; answer signal = A



Question 22: a reference to wedding property workforce economy favours training

Correct Answer: A

Accepted Answers: A

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: She argues that the old sequence of school, job, marriage and home no longer fits a labour market that rewards extended preparation.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ A เพราะ statement นี้ตรงกับคำพูดของ Dr Lena Ortiz ที่ว่า old sequence of school, job, marriage and home no longer fits a labour market that rewards extended preparation โจทย์ใช้คำว่า traditional order และ current labour market ซึ่ง paraphrase จาก old sequence และ labour market ใน passage จึงเลือก A

Paraphrased Vocabulary: workforce economy favours training = she argues old sequence school job marriage home; answer signal = A



Question 23: a reference to costs down payment

Correct Answer: B

Accepted Answers: B

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: Professor Mark Feld, a housing economist, points out that in many cities rents have risen faster than wages, making it harder to save for a deposit.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ B เพราะ statement นี้กล่าวถึง high rents และ difficulty of saving for a deposit ซึ่งเป็นความเห็นของ Professor Mark Feld ในย่อหน้า C เขาบอกว่า rents have risen faster than wages และทำให้ harder to save for a deposit อีกทั้งเขาย้ำว่า delayed home ownership เกี่ยวกับ prices, debt and insecure earnings มากกว่า lifestyle preference จึงตอบ B

Paraphrased Vocabulary: costs down payment = professor mark feld housing economist points out many; answer signal = B



Question 24: a reference to wedding dedication

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: Sociologist Maya Singh argues that later marriage should not automatically be interpreted as fear of commitment. Many young adults, she says, want partnerships based on emotional maturity, equality and shared domestic work.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะ statement นี้ตรงกับ Maya Singh ที่บอกว่า later marriage ไม่ควรถูกตีความทันทีว่า fear of commitment และคนหนุ่มสาวต้องการ partnerships ที่มี emotional maturity, equality และ shared domestic work คำว่า equal relationships ในโจทย์เทียบกับ equality and shared domestic work ใน passage จึงตอบ C

Paraphrased Vocabulary: wedding dedication = sociologist maya singh argues later marriage automatically interpreted; answer signal = C



Question 25: a reference to how access to affordable nursery care and statutory leave shapes timing of parenthood

Correct Answer: C

Accepted Answers: C

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: She also observes that reliable childcare and parental leave policies affect decisions about when to have children.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ C เพราะ statement นี้กล่าวถึง decisions about children และ care systems ซึ่งตรงกับ Maya Singh ที่สังเกตว่า reliable childcare and parental leave policies affect decisions about when to have children คำว่า care systems ในโจทย์สรุปจาก childcare and parental leave policies ดังนั้นผู้พูดคือ C

Paraphrased Vocabulary: care support parental leave = she observes reliable childcare parental leave policies affect; answer signal = C



Question 26: a reference to workforce economy piecemeal time off secure

Correct Answer: D

Accepted Answers: D

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 2 - Delayed Adulthood

Exact Portion: Dr Jonah Reed, a labour-market researcher, says that early employment has become more fragmented. Short contracts, platform work and repeated job changes can leave young people employed but not settled.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ D เพราะ statement นี้ใช้คำสำคัญจากความเห็นของ Dr Jonah Reed โดยตรง ย่อหน้า E ระบุว่า early employment has become more fragmented และ short contracts, platform work, repeated job changes ทำให้ young people employed but not settled ดังนั้นคำตอบคือ D ไม่ใช่คนอื่นที่พูดเรื่อง education, housing หรือ relationships

Paraphrased Vocabulary: piecemeal time off secure = jonah reed labour market researcher says early employment; answer signal = D
